skill.name: webapp-testing
skill.description: Toolkit for interacting with and testing local web applications using Playwright. Supports verifying frontend functionality, debugging UI behavior, capturing browser screenshots, and viewing browser console logs.
skill.summary: Test local web applications with Playwright automation and server management helpers.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 42 patterns but all are false positives. LICENSE.txt URLs are standard Apache 2.0 license references. 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings incorrectly flagged license text and argparse code. subprocess usage in with_server.py is legitimate for server management. SKILL.md code blocks are documentation examples, not executable code. Skill is safe for publication.

low_findings.title: Subprocess execution with shell=True
low_findings.description: with_server.py uses subprocess.Popen with shell=True to start development servers. This is expected behavior for a server management helper script that needs to support commands with cd and && operators.

content.user_title: Test web applications with Playwright automation
content.value_statement: Local web application testing requires managing servers and automating browser interactions. This skill provides Playwright scripts and server helpers to streamline frontend testing workflows.
actual_capabilities: Start and manage local development servers with automatic port monitoring
Write Playwright scripts to interact with web applications
Capture browser screenshots for visual debugging
Inspect rendered DOM and discover element selectors
Monitor browser console logs during test execution
Support multiple concurrent servers for full-stack testing
limitations: Only works with local web applications, not remote URLs
Requires Node.js or Python runtime for server commands
Test scripts must use synchronous Playwright API
Server startup timeout defaults to 30 seconds per server

use_cases.title: Frontend Developer Testing UI Components
use_cases.description: Automate testing of React or Vue components by starting the dev server and verifying UI behavior with Playwright scripts.

use_cases.title: Full-Stack Integration Testing
use_cases.description: Run backend API server and frontend dev server simultaneously, then execute end-to-end tests against the integrated application.

use_cases.title: Visual Regression Detection
use_cases.description: Capture screenshots of web application states and compare visually to detect unintended UI changes during development.

prompt_templates.title: Basic HTML Element Discovery
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the webapp-testing skill to inspect a local HTML file and find all button elements. Navigate to the file, wait for the page to load, then list all buttons with their text content.

prompt_templates.title: Dev Server Automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Start a React dev server on port 3000 using with_server.py, then write a Playwright script that navigates to the app, clicks the login button, and verifies the dashboard appears.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Server Integration Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Use with_server.py to start a Python Flask backend on port 5000 and a Vite frontend on port 5173. Write a test that submits a form on the frontend and verifies the data appears in the backend response.

prompt_templates.title: Visual Debugging Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Navigate to a local webapp, wait for networkidle, capture a full-page screenshot to /tmp/debug.png, and extract the page content HTML. Identify all input fields and their associated labels.

output_examples.input: Discover all interactive elements on a login page
output: Found 3 input fields: email (type=email), password (type=password), remember (type=checkbox)
Screenshot saved to /tmp/login_page.png showing form layout

output_examples.input: Test form submission with server running

best_practices: Always wait for networkidle state before inspecting dynamic web applications
Use the with_server.py helper as a black box - run --help first to understand usage
Capture screenshots before DOM inspection for visual debugging context
anti_patterns: Inspecting the DOM before waiting for networkidle on JavaScript-heavy applications
Reading script source code into context when --help provides sufficient usage information
Using file:// URLs for dynamic applications that require a running server

faq.question: Do I need to install Playwright separately?
faq.answer: Yes, install Playwright with 'pip install playwright' and run 'playwright install' to download browser binaries before using this skill.

faq.question: How do I test a static HTML file without a server?
faq.answer: Use file:// URLs directly in your Playwright script. Navigate to 'file:///path/to/file.html' without using the with_server.py helper.

faq.question: Can I test multiple servers at once?
faq.answer: Yes, with_server.py supports multiple --server and --port pairs. Each server starts sequentially and waits for readiness before starting the next.

faq.question: What if my server takes longer than 30 seconds to start?
faq.answer: Use the --timeout argument to increase the wait time. Example: --timeout 60 for 60 seconds per server.

faq.question: Why use headless mode for browser testing?
faq.answer: Headless mode runs faster and works in environments without a display. Remove headless=True if you need to visually observe browser behavior.

faq.question: How do I debug failing tests?
faq.answer: Use page.screenshot() to capture the page state at failure points. Enable browser console logging to see JavaScript errors. Run with headless=False to observe browser behavior visually.
